title: Pinalia wildgrubeana
intro: Pinalia wildgrubeana, also known as Wildgrube's Pinalia, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Pinalia wildgrubeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pinalia wildgrubeana is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension and respiratory infections.

This genus Pinalia is a group of orchids native to tropical regions, primarily in Asia and the Pacific Islands.
